definition_population.content: SLE is a chronic, relapsing-remitting systemic autoimmune disease driven by loss of tolerance to nuclear self-antigens, causing autoantibody/immune-complex damage across organs; predominantly affects women of childbearing age.

epidemiology.content: Global modelling study (112 studies) estimated country/region-specific SLE incidence and prevalence. Pediatric-onset SLE (cSLE) is a recognized subgroup studied in a 182-patient belimumab cohort.

sources.quote: To quantify global, regional and country-specific estimates of epidemiology of systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE).
This retrospective study included 182 patients with cSLE treated with belimumab

biology.content: FOXN2 has no PubMed-indexed literature link to lupus (0 hits on direct search) — not established in this pass. Peripheral gut-microbiome metaproteomic dysregulation has been reported in SLE patients, but this is not a validated CD4+ T-cell-intrinsic mechanism tying to the platform target.

sources.quote: we utilized quantitative metaproteomics to analyze fecal samples from 103 SLE patients and 62 healthy controls

treatment.content: Belimumab (BENLYSTA), an anti-BLyS/BAFF monoclonal antibody, is FDA-approved for SLE (BLA125370, original approval 2011-03-09) and remains the only biologic approved for childhood-onset SLE.

sources.source_url: (no URL returned by tool; MCP drug-regulatory record: application_number=BLA125370, sponsor=HUMAN GENOME SCIENCES INC.)
sources.quote: BLA125370, sponsor HUMAN GENOME SCIENCES INC., original approval action 2011-03-09
Belimumab is the only biologic approved for childhood-onset systemic lupus erythematosus (cSLE)

regulatory_trials.content: ClinicalTrials.gov lists 1,086 total interventional trials registered under the condition 'systemic lupus erythematosus'.

gaps: FOXN2-specific mechanistic link to SLE or CD4+ T-cell biology not established in this pass (0 PubMed hits).
No FDA drug-label or trial-level analysis was pulled beyond the belimumab application summary.
